title: sj-audit walk plan + result
description: Two shapes. The PLAN (this schema's root) is authored by the orchestrator AFTER live exploration and fed to scripts/walk/run-walks.mjs. The RESULT ($defs.walkResult) is what the runner writes to walks.json — one entry per walk with the executed outcome. The plan is behavioral intent in user terms; the result is what actually happened when a first-time user (the driver) attempted it.

walks.description: Author 1 walk per high-value user story (top personas x core jobs). Prefer stories that CROSS a populated/error/multi-step boundary the static screenshots can't reach (create something, complete a task, recover from an error, find a feature).

target.description: How to find an element, in user-perceivable terms first. A bare string resolves to role=button|link|tab|menuitem|text (in that order). Use an object for precision; an array tries each in order (resilient to copy drift).

role.description: ARIA role, e.g. button, link, tab, textbox

action.description: press/key take a key name in value (e.g. 'Enter'); wait takes ms in value; goto takes a path/url in value

value.description: for fill/type (text), press/key (key name), goto (path), wait (ms)

note.description: the user-intent label for this step — shows in the report timeline and screenshot name

optional.description: if true, a failure here does NOT mark the walk stuck — the runner continues (use for dismissable nags, cookie banners)

timeout.description: per-step ms budget (default --timeout). A non-optional step that times out = the user got STUCK here.

settle.description: ms to wait after the action before screenshotting (default 500)

type.description: text=visible text present; selector=CSS present; url=current URL contains; absent=text NOT present

story.description: As a <persona>, I want <goal>, so that <benefit>.

goal.description: plain-language definition of done — what proves the task succeeded

viewport.description: WxH, e.g. 390x844 to walk the story on mobile

success.description: ALL must pass for outcome=completed. Omit only for pure-exploration walks.

walkResult.description: Written by run-walks.mjs to walks.json under { base, walks:[walkResult], summary }. Read by the workflow (analyst grounding + reconciliation) and the report.

outcome.description: completed=goal reached; stuck=a required step's target never became actionable (discoverability/dead-end); failed=steps ran but goal not confirmed or an error surfaced; error=harness fault, not a product verdict

deadEnd.description: present when stuck: { atStep, url, shot, interactive:[{tag,role,name,w,h,small}] } — what the user could see/click at the dead-end, with hit-target sizes
